name: Abis ibn Abi Shabib
title: The Armorless Lover
shortDescription: A fearless Arab warrior whose love for Hussain (AS) intoxicated him so much that he took off his armor in the battlefield.
fullStory:
Abis ibn Abi Shabib al-Shakiri was a prominent elder, orator, and brave Shia warrior in Kufa. The Bani Shakir tribe was famous among the Arabs for their bravery, and Abis was considered one of their best. He was the first person to pledge allegiance to Muslim ibn Aqeel in Mukhtar's house upon Muslim's arrival in Kufa, and he delivered Muslim's letters from Kufa to Imam Hussain (AS) in Mecca.

Abis's love and devotion to Imam Hussain (AS) were absolute. On the day of Ashura, after the martyrdom of many companions, he came to the Imam with his loyal servant 'Shawthab', bid him farewell, and entered the battlefield. Abis began reciting war poetry and challenged anyone to single combat.

The men of the Kufan army, well aware of Abis's bravery and combat skills, did not dare to face him alone. Umar ibn Sa'd, seeing the helplessness of his army, ordered: 'Stone him!'. A shower of stones rained down on Abis from all sides.

According to the report of Tarikh al-Tabari, Abis, seeing this cowardice and overwhelmed by the eagerness to meet his Lord and his love for Imam Hussain (AS), took off his helmet and armor, threw them aside, and charged into the heart of the 30,000-strong enemy army bare and armorless with only his sword.

A Kufan narrator says: 'By God, I saw Abis single-handedly scatter over two hundred soldiers, and they were fleeing from him'. Finally, due to the heavy stoning and multiple blows from all sides, this brave lover was martyred defending Wilayah, and he was beheaded.
quote: The love of Hussain (AS) has driven me mad! (While taking off his armor)
